name: nats.py (NATS Messaging for Python)

auth.notes: NATS credentials: token, username/password, NKey, or NATS credentials file

performance.notes: NATS is extremely fast — sub-millisecond for local; 1-10ms distributed

agent_readiness.notes: Async Python client for NATS messaging server. import nats. nc = await nats.connect('nats://localhost:4222'). await nc.publish('subject', b'message'). sub = await nc.subscribe('subject', cb=message_handler). async def message_handler(msg): print(msg.data). Request-reply: reply = await nc.request('service', b'data', timeout=1). JetStream: js = nc.jetstream(); await js.publish('stream.subject', b'data'); await js.subscribe('stream.*', cb=handler). Key-Value: kv = await js.create_key_value(KeyValueConfig('bucket')); await kv.put('key', b'value'); val = await kv.get('key'). Object store: os = await js.create_object_store(). Queues: sub = await nc.subscribe('subject', queue='workers'). Drain and close: await nc.drain().

gotchas: async-only in nats.py 2.x — no sync client
NATS server must be running separately (docker or binary)
JetStream persistence requires JetStream-enabled NATS server
Message acknowledgment in JetStream must be explicit
Connection string format: nats://host:4222 (not URL-encoded by default)
best_when: Microservice pub/sub messaging with low latency
JetStream for persistent message queues with NATS
avoid_when: Kafka-scale data streaming (use Kafka/aiokafka for millions/sec)
When persistence and replay are primary concerns (use Kafka or RabbitMQ)
summary: nats.py is an async Python client for NATS messaging. nc = await nats.connect(); await nc.publish('subject', b'data'); sub = await nc.subscribe('subject', cb=handler). Request/reply: await nc.request(). JetStream: js.publish(), js.subscribe(). Key-Value store, object store. Queue groups for load balancing. Apache-2.0 by NATS.io. async-only in v2. Best for low-latency microservice pub/sub. NATS server required separately.
